mapping.a_element: stationary transonic accretion flow being perturbed
mapping.b_element: coherent plane-wave light background being perturbed

mapping.a_element: linear perturbation added to the stationary solution
mapping.b_element: modulational sideband perturbation of the wave

brief_justification: The only overlap is the generic act of linearly perturbing a stationary state and asking whether the perturbation grows, which the guidance flags as too generic to count. A's actual machinery is dynamical-systems classification of transonic critical points (saddle/centre) plus an acoustic-geometry perturbation equation that concludes stability, whereas B's is a modulational-instability dispersion relation in a Kerr medium whose unstable band is reshaped by the incoherence spectrum; there is no counterpart in A for the incoherence spectrum or Kerr nonlinearity, nor any counterpart in B for the critical-point classification, so the driving mechanisms differ.
